nodes.x: Why do the heathen rage, and the people imagine a vain thing?

Why do the Heathen rage, and the people imagine a vain thing, the rulers take counsel together, against the Lord, and against his anointed. He that sitteth in the heavens shall laugh; the Lord 6hall have them in derision. And break them with a rod of iron ; and dash them in peices like a potters vessel. — D avid.
Who by the mouth of thy servant David hast said, Why did the heathen rage, and the people imagine vain things?
Behold, all ye yourselves have seen it; why then are ye thus altogether vain?

David understood this matter. He exclaims, “ Why do the heathen rage, and the people imagine a vain thing ? The kings of the earth and the rulers take counsel against the Lord and against His anointed. The word heathen is commonly associated with the idea of black skins, flat noses, and woolly heads. But it does not follow that a heathen must necessarily be clad in a dark skiD. There are heathens…
And again, The Lord knoweth the thoughts of the wise, that they are vain.
The kings of the earth set themselves, and the rulers take counsel together, against the Lord, and against his anointed, saying,
The kings of the earth stood up, and the rulers were gathered together against the Lord, and against his Christ.
All the kings of the earth shall praise thee, O Lord, when they hear the words of thy mouth.
Kings of the earth, and all people; princes, and all judges of the earth:
Be wise now therefore, O ye kings: be instructed, ye judges of the earth.
Let us break their bands asunder, and cast away their cords from us.
Then I cut asunder mine other staff, even Bands, that I might break the brotherhood between Judah and Israel.

And the chief judge stood before them, and smote them again, and said unto them: If ye have the power of God deliver yourselves from these bands, and then we will believe that the Lord will destroy this people according to your words.
Yea, wo be unto you because of that great abomination which has come among you; and ye have united yourselves unto it, yea, to that secret band which was established by Gadianton!
He that sitteth in the heavens shall laugh: the Lord shall have them in derision.
But thou, O Lord, shalt laugh at them; thou shalt have all the heathen in derision.

midst of Israel; and when the heathen rage, and the people imagine a vain thing, and the rulers take counsel together against the Lord and against His anointed, then “He that sitteth in the Heavens shall laugh at their calamity: the Lord shall have them in derision.” Amen.
The righteous also shall see, and fear, and shall laugh at him:
The Lord shall laugh at him: for he seeth that his day is coming.
Then shall he speak unto them in his wrath, and vex them in his sore displeasure.
Then shall he say also unto them on the left hand, Depart from me, ye cursed, into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels:
This we will do to them; we will even let them live, lest wrath be upon us, because of the oath which we sware unto them.
And they spake unto him, saying, If thou be kind to this people, and please them, and speak good words to them, they will be thy servants for ever.
And behold, he shall say unto you, Nay.
Then shall they cry unto the Lord, but he will not hear them: he will even hide his face from them at that time, as they have behaved themselves ill in their doings.
Yet have I set my king upon my holy hill of Zion.
Behold, the land of Zion—I, the Lord, hold it in mine own hands;
So shall ye know that I am the Lord your God dwelling in Zion, my holy mountain: then shall Jerusalem be holy, and there shall no strangers pass through her any more.
I will declare the decree: the Lord hath said unto me, Thou art my Son; this day have I begotten thee.
But verily I say unto you, that I have decreed a decree which my people shall realize, inasmuch as they hearken from this very hour unto the counsel which I, the Lord their God, shall give unto them.
And now I have this day declared it to you; but ye have not obeyed the voice of the Lord your God, nor any thing for the which he hath sent me unto you.

Thus saith the Lord; for I am God, and have sent mine Only Begotten Son into the world for the redemption of the world, and have decreed that he that receiveth him shall be saved, and he that receiveth him not shall be damned—
Ask of me, and I shall give thee the heathen for thine inheritance, and the uttermost parts of the earth for thy possession.
And thou shalt take thine inheritance in thyself in the sight of the heathen, and thou shalt know that I am the Lord.
And it shall be, when thou art come in unto the land which the Lord thy God giveth thee for an inheritance, and possessest it, and dwellest therein;
And I will scatter thee among the heathen, and disperse thee in the countries, and will consume thy filthiness out of thee.
That which is altogether just shalt thou follow, that thou mayest live, and inherit the land which the Lord thy God giveth thee.
And after a few years, if thou desirest of me, thou mayest go up also unto the goodly land, to possess thine inheritance;
Thou shalt break them with a rod of iron; thou shalt dash them in pieces like a potter’s vessel.
And there shalt thou build an altar unto the Lord thy God, an altar of stones: thou shalt not lift up any iron tool upon them.
And he shall rule them with a rod of iron; as the vessels of a potter shall they be broken to shivers: even as I received of my Father.
And thou shalt put the staves into the rings by the sides of the ark, that the ark may be borne with them.
And when the Lord thy God shall deliver them before thee; thou shalt smite them, and utterly destroy them; thou shalt make no covenant with them, nor shew mercy unto them:
Thou shalt make them as a fiery oven in the time of thine anger: the Lord shall swallow them up in his wrath, and the fire shall devour them.
Be wise now therefore, O ye kings: be instructed, ye judges of the earth.

Therefore I will be your king the remainder of my days; nevertheless, let us appoint judges, to judge this people according to our law; and we will newly arrange the affairs of this people, for we will appoint wise men to be judges, that will judge this people according to the commandments of God.
¶ Then the king said to the wise men, which knew the times, (for so was the king’s manner toward all that knew law and judgment:
Behold, a king shall reign in righteousness, and princes shall rule in judgment.
The kings of the earth set themselves, and the rulers take counsel together, against the Lord, and against his anointed, saying,
Serve the Lord with fear, and rejoice with trembling.
Sanctify the Lord of hosts himself; and let him be your fear, and let him be your dread.
Sanctify the Lord of Hosts himself, and let him be your fear, and let him be your dread.
For thus saith the Lord; We have heard a voice of trembling, of fear, and not of peace.
Be strong and of a good courage, fear not, nor be afraid of them: for the Lord thy God, he it is that doth go with thee; he will not fail thee, nor forsake thee.
Servants, be obedient to them that are your masters according to the flesh, with fear and trembling, in singleness of your heart, as unto Christ;
Kiss the Son, lest he be angry, and ye perish from the way, when his wrath is kindled but a little. Blessed are all they that put their trust in him.
Having yet therefore one son, his wellbeloved, he sent him also last unto them, saying, They will reverence my son.
But last of all he sent unto them his son, saying, They will reverence my son.
A wise son maketh a glad father: but a foolish man despiseth his mother.
For they will turn away thy son from following me, that they may serve other gods: so will the anger of the Lord be kindled against you, and destroy thee suddenly.
